core_payload.content:
# N-Infinity Parliament

## 1. Future Role
Dream keeps Parliament as the next deliberation layer above the current real-side triad arbitration runtime. The real capsule remains canonical for today's governed proposer-critic-synthesizer flow; Dream only preserves the future delta that could make contradiction handling more inspectable, more graduated, and calmer at higher graph scale.

## 2. Future Delta
- add stronger docketing and escalation tiers so contradiction cases can be grouped by severity instead of handled as isolated one-offs
- improve evidence bundles, comparative verdict explanations, and quarantine handoff packaging for human review
- support richer multi-case and cross-cluster arbitration when tension spans more than one pair of claims
- make middle-path preservation and unresolved tension more explicit so Parliament can leave durable debate structure instead of forced closure
- deepen the relationship between Parliament, Weaver, and future suggestion lanes without implying a new live runtime already exists

## 3. Why Keep Dream
Future graph scale will increase contradiction pressure, not remove it. Parliament therefore still needs a future doctrine for broader deliberation, better review ergonomics, and more structured governance escalation once the current bounded runtime hits its ceiling.

## 4. Guardrails
Dream does not replace the current TypeScript Parliament runtime, does not imply silent auto-quarantine authority, and does not restate today's live triad arbitration path as if it were still only speculative doctrine.

neuro_concentrate.summary: N-Infinity Parliament in Dream now keeps only the future deliberation delta above the current real-side arbitration runtime. Real remains canonical for the live proposer-critic-synthesizer flow, evidence-weighted verdicting, draft resolution capsule output, and local gate reporting that exist today. Dream instead preserves the next layer: richer docketing and escalation tiers, better evidence and quarantine handoff bundles, broader cross-cluster arbitration, and clearer preservation of unresolved tension when a conflict should stay open. This keeps future epistemic-governance ambition visible without pretending the current triad runtime is still only a planned institution.
